frameworks.name: executive attention theory
frameworks.one_line: Domain-free goal-directed attention underlies working memory and higher cognition.

frameworks.name: working memory capacity
frameworks.one_line: WMC reflects attentional resource allocation tied to fluid intelligence.

frameworks.name: fluid intelligence
frameworks.one_line: Gf measures share attentional substrate with WMC tasks.

core_question: How do individual differences in attention control account for variation in working memory capacity and fluid intelligence?
key_assumptions: attentional resources are domain-free and goal-directed
maintenance and disengagement share an executive substrate
WMC and Gf tap a common attentional pool
key_findings: individual variation in maintenance and disengagement co-varies
WMC-Gf correlation traces to shared attentional resource demands
interpretations: executive attention is the common cause of WMC-Gf covariation
WMC and Gf reflect different allocations of the same attentional system
limitations_noted: theoretical alternatives to executive attention remain viable
empirical complications in measuring attention control
